steps.explanation: The sample estimate is 27.8 oz and the margin of error is 0.02 oz. The margin of error creates an interval around the estimated mean, not around individual bag weights.
Lower bound: 27.8 - 0.02 = 27.78 oz. Upper bound: 27.8 + 0.02 = 27.82 oz. The interval [27.78, 27.82] oz is the plausible range for the true population mean.

• A: The mean weight of all handbags is between 27.78 oz and 27.82 oz — This correctly applies the margin of error to the estimated mean. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: The actual weights of all handbags are between 27.78 oz and 27.82 oz — Margin of error describes uncertainty about the mean, not the spread of individual weights. Eliminated.
  • C: The actual weights of all handbags from the sample are between 27.78 oz and 27.82 oz — Same error as B; margin of error is not a bound on individual observations. Eliminated.
  • D: The mean weight is exactly 27.8 oz — This ignores the margin of error and treats the estimate as certain. Eliminated.
